channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: On their way! 4 people on NASA Crew-12 mission launch to International Space Station
item.description: The four are set to dock with the space station on Saturday, returning the orbital lab to its full complement of seven. NASA's last mission, Crew-11, ...

item.title: Minnesota immigration crackdown will end, border czar says. And, DHS funding to expire
item.description: Border czar Tom Homan announced that the Trump administration will end the immigration crackdown in Minnesota. And, DHS funding is set to expire after...

item.title: Who is the 'Quad God' and what does he want? The quiz holds the answers you seek
item.description: Plus: more Olympics, the Super Bowl and some monks.

item.title: Britain's High Court says government illegally banned Pro-Palestinian group
item.description: In its ruling, the court said an earlier decision to ban the Pro-Palestinian group Palestine Action as a terrorist organization was "disproportionate....

item.title: Who will police Gaza, and how?
item.description: Under President Trump's Gaza ceasefire plan, Arab countries and the European Union are supposed to train a new police force in the Gaza Strip. But U.S...

item.title: Journalist Don Lemon is set to be arraigned in Minnesota church protest case
item.description: Former CNN host Don Lemon and four other people are set to be arraigned on federal civil rights charges in Minnesota, accused in a protest at a church...

item.title: Signature Puerto Rico drink recipes
item.description: Bad Bunny's Super Bowl halftime show has led to a huge spike in interest in his music and Puerto Rican culture. Dorian González Vega, the beverage dir...

item.title: Leak damages 19th Century painting in latest Louvre setback
item.description: A catalogue of incidents at the world's most visited museum have placed museum bosses come under increasing scrutiny.

item.title: JD Vance to join Tony Dokoupil for CBS News "Things That Matter" town hall
item.description: Vice President JD Vance will talk about the economy, foreign policy, the state of the Republican Party and the 2028 race for the White House in a Marc...

item.title: Norwegian biathlete who admitted to cheating on girlfriend wins second medal
item.description: Strula Holm Laegreid of Norway revealed in a post-race interview earlier this week that he had been unfaithful to his girlfriend but hoped to win her ...

item.title: "Sinners" cinematographer opens up about career journey amid historic Oscar nomination
item.description: "Sinners" cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw says she doesn't want the audience to notice her work because "you wanna make it so good it feels like...

item.title: Rubio warns Europe of new era in geopolitics before big Munich speech
item.description: The US Secretary of State will address the first major transatlantic meeting since Donald Trump threatened to annex Greenland.

item.title: Romance costs more this year as inflation lifts Valentine's Day gift prices
item.description: Love is biting consumers this year amid the rising cost of flowers, chocolates and other Valentine's Day staples.

item.title: Affordable housing residents near Portland ICE building to ask judge to limit feds’ use of tear gas
Watch SpaceX rocket blast off to International Space Station
item.description: Watch moment Falcon 9 rocket blasts off to International Space Station

item.title: Bangladesh Nationalist Party sweeps to victory in first election since Gen Z uprising
item.description: Voters are hoping for a return to democracy after 15 years of authoritarian rule under Sheikh Hasina.

item.title: Prosecutors to dismiss charges against men accused of assaulting ICE officer in Minneapolis
Watch: How Andrew's BBC interview compares to what Epstein emails tell us now
item.description: Following the latest release of the Epstein files, claims made by the then Prince Andrew in 2019 are under fresh scrutiny.
